{{Quote|The X-Ones were ''defense'' robots, programmed to automatically fight against any hostile act!|C-3PO, on the Ranger X-1|<ref name="The Destroyer" />}}
The '''Ranger X-1''' was an antiquated model<ref name="The Droids Re-Animated, Part 1" /> of [[Gender/Legends|masculine]]<ref name="The Destroyer" /> [[Bodyguard droid/Legends|defense droid]]<ref name="The Droids Re-Animated, Part 1" /> programmed to automatically fight any act of hostility. It had [[Color/Legends|green]] plating with "X-1" emblazoned in white on its chest, a pair of white [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]], and morphed into a box when deactivated. The series was terminated when the [[Intergalactic Law Agency]] was founded<ref name="The Destroyer" /> during the last [[Standard century/Legends|century]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|107|Intergalactic Law Agency}}</ref> and the last of the X-1s were collected and scrapped.<ref name="The Destroyer" />

However, a Ranger X-1 brought to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dodz]] to protect pioneers was missed by the ILA and found by [[Jost Ellon]] near the world's [[crystal mines]]. After meeting [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], Ellon discovered that the X-1 had corroded [[Power cell/Legends|power cells]] and kicked the inert [[Droid/Legends|droid]], accidentally reactivating it. Despite being low on power, Ellon wanted to use the X-1 to aid the merchants of Dodz against [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Kugg]], who used his [[Battle droid/Legends|killer droid]], the [[Destroyer (droid)|Destroyer]],<ref name="The Destroyer" /> to [[Death|murder]]<ref name="The Droids Re-Animated, Part 1" /> those who refused to pay his heavy taxes. Although outmatched, the Ranger was able to destroy the Destroyer when R2 tapped into its power and fed it back into the X-1.<ref name="The Destroyer" /> The defense droid ran out of energy in a permanently heroic pose,<ref name="The Droids Re-Animated, Part 1" /> remaining on top of a hill as a reminder of its importance to the people of Dodz for ending Kugg's corrupt reign.<ref name="The Destroyer" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Ranger X-1 first appeared in [[Droids (1986) 1|''Droids'' (1986) 1]], the first issue of the [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars Droids'']] comic series published by [[Star Comics]], an imprint of [[Marvel Comics]],<ref name="The Destroyer" /> which was first published in [[February]] [[1986]].<ref name="Marvel Age">''[[Marvel Age]]'' 36</ref>
